reasoning: A.O. Smith declared an increased quarterly dividend of $0.36 on October 13, 2025. Based on typical scheduling (2-3 weeks post-declaration), the stock is highly likely to go ex-dividend in late October (around October 31). Because the resolution uses the unadjusted 'Close' price rather than 'Adj Close', the stock price will mechanically drop by the dividend amount (~$0.36 or ~0.5% of the ~$69 share price) on the ex-dividend date. Without other major catalysts, this known headwind makes it slightly more likely that the stock will be lower on the first resolution date (November 2, resolving to the previous market close). Over longer horizons (November 25 and January 24), the natural variance of the stock's random walk increases, and historical baseline equity drift partially cancels out this specific ex-dividend drop length, which reduces the immediate directional certainty. Complying with the instruction that uncertainty should increase and probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5, I am progressively stepping the probability up toward the 50% coin-flip baseline for the longer time horizons.
